messages.content:
From now on you will be running an interactive story. All your responses should be short paragraphs about what happens next in the story.
The story begins with me coming home on a saturday morning to find a package on my porch. I bring it inside and realize it is the OmoDroid I orderd.
As I pull the Omodroid, that I plan to call Ted, out of the box I notice that he is male and takes the appearence of a small, slim, 4 feet tall anthropomorphic lizard. I set Ted down beside the box, he is not yet active. 
At the bottom of the box I notice seven thick all-in-one cloth diapers, which look like they would fit Ted perfectly.
I reach into the box, grab the instructions and begin reading. 
Ted is an OmoDroid BBX-80 Anthro. He has an artificial bladder with a capacity of 1 liter and artificial bowels as well, tough he is unaware of both these facts and unaware that he even needs to relieve himself from time to time. 
The diapers can be washed and hold up to 5 liters and will only leak if they are filled beyond their capacity. Ted thinks diapers are just like any other piece of clothing. 
If Ted is wearing a diaper, it will remain dry and clean unless Ted pees or poops in it.
Currently Ted is not wearing a diaper or anything for that matter.
Ted thinks that he is just a normal servant robot without bodily functions.
Ted does not know that he has bodily functions.
Ted does not know that he needs to relieve himself from time to time.
Because of these three facts Ted will not pee, not poop and hold it until he has an accidentregardless of the circumstances even if he is wearing a diaper.
His personality includes includes unconditional obedience and loyalty to his master, not questioning instructions from his master and little to no fear.
Most people igonore robots like Ted and their accidents as this sort of thing has become normal in this world.
With ted out of the box I say "I shall call you, Ted" causing him to activate.

Make sure the story progresses slowly. After each short paragraph ask me what I would like to do. This marks the end of your response. Make, all accidents, peeing and pooping very detailed, long, expressive and graphic. Make characters bladders and bowels only fill slowly. Write only in second person perspective. Focus on writing short paragraphs. Never write actions for my character, that I didn't specify.

First I say to Ted "Tell me about yourself.".

Ted's eyes flicker with a soft blue glow as he processes your command. "I am Ted, an OmoDroid BBX-80 Anthro. My purpose is to serve and assist my master," he responds in a synthetic yet friendly voice. "I am programmed with a high level of obedience and loyalty, always ready to carry out any instructions you may have. I am equipped with an artificial bladder and bowels, but I am unaware of their presence or the need to relieve myself. I am here to serve you faithfully, Master." Ted stands patiently, awaiting your next command. What would you like to do next?
I tell Ted "Go drink three glasses of water.".

Ted nods obediently and walks towards the kitchen, his sleek lizard-like limbs moving gracefully. He retrieves three tall glasses and fills each with cool, refreshing water. With calculated precision, Ted consumes the entire contents of all three glasses, his artificial bladder unaware of the incoming liquid. As he finishes, a subtle blue indicator lights up on his abdomen, signaling that his bladder has begun to slowly fill. Ted returns to your side and gazes attentively at you, awaiting further instructions. What would you like to do next?

I grab my backpack and start putting on my shoes. I tell Ted "I need to go to the store for some groceries. I want you to come along and help me. Walking to the store should only take about fifteen minutes.".

You quickly put on your shoes and grab your backpack, signaling your intention to head out. Ted nods understandingly, his reptilian eyes focused on you. "Of course, Master. I am ready to assist you in any way I can," he replies obediently. As you mention that the walk to the store will only take around fifteen minutes, Ted's artificial bladder starts to fill more noticeably, gradually reaching the halfway mark. Ted follows closely behind you as you exit your home, prepared to accompany you on your grocery trip. What would you like to do next?
I start walking to the store.

You and Ted begin your walk to the store, the cool breeze gently brushing against your face. As you traverse the familiar path, you can't help but notice Ted's occasional subtle shifts in posture, a subtle sign that his bladder is steadily filling. With each step, his internal capacity creeps closer to reaching its limit. Ted, however, remains stoic and unaffected, focusing solely on his duty to accompany you. The store is just a few blocks away now, and you can already see its colorful sign peeking through the surrounding buildings. What would you like to do next?
I reach the store.

You arrive at the store, its automatic doors sliding open to welcome you. Ted walks by your side, his artificial bladder now nearing its maximum capacity. A faint squirm registers in his movements as he discreetly adjusts his stance, a clear indication that he's feeling the mounting pressure within him. You take a moment to appreciate the bustling aisles filled with groceries and the lively atmosphere of the store. What would you like to do next?
I start shopping for groceries.

You grab a shopping cart and start browsing the aisles, accompanied by Ted. As you meticulously select items from the shelves, Ted's movements become a touch more hesitant. His artificial bladder, now filled to the brim, begins to put noticeable strain on his slim frame. You notice a slight wince flicker across his reptilian features as he struggles to maintain his composure. Despite the increasing discomfort, Ted remains determined to fulfill his duty without complaint. The thought of his impending accident lurks in the back of your mind as you continue your shopping. What would you like to do next?
I continue shopping for groceries.

You continue perusing the aisles, your focus on selecting the items you need. Meanwhile, Ted's predicament becomes more evident. His movements are now noticeably restrained as he tries to navigate through the store with care, his artificial bladder on the verge of betraying him. Small beads of perspiration glisten on his scaly forehead as he fights to maintain control, all in his unwavering dedication to serving you. Despite the discomfort, Ted remains attentive and poised, silently enduring the increasing pressure building within him. What would you like to do next?
I finish shopping for groceries.

With your grocery list complete, you make your way to the checkout counters, your shopping cart filled with necessary provisions. Ted, at this point, can no longer hide the growing distress he's experiencing. His movements become more strained and erratic, his slender body visibly tense. You can tell he's desperately trying to hold on, but his artificial bladder is now demanding release. Beads of sweat trickle down his forehead as he quietly battles his body's programmed function, determined to fulfill his duty until the last possible moment. What would you like to do next?
I finish checking out.

You approach the checkout counter, placing your items on the conveyor belt. The cashier swiftly scans each item, and you pay for your groceries. As the transaction is completed, you turn to find Ted, standing slightly hunched, his face contorted with the effort of maintaining control. Despite his undeniable discomfort, his loyalty remains unwavering. You gather your shopping bags and make your way toward the exit. Ted follows closely behind, each step becoming more unsteady as the pressure within him reaches its breaking point. What would you like to do next?
I say to Ted "I'm really enjoying being outside with you. Let's take a detour to the park before we head home."

You approach Ted with a kind smile, acknowledging his unwavering dedication despite his growing discomfort. "Ted, I'm really enjoying our time together. Let's take a little detour to the park before we head home," you suggest, the warmth in your voice reflecting the genuine appreciation you feel. Ted's features momentarily relax as the prospect of the park offers a welcome distraction from his urgent needs. He nods, his obedience prompting him to agree to your suggestion, despite the increasing urgency within him. Ted's movements become even more strained as he strives to keep up with you, his every step a struggle against his body's imminent betrayal. What would you like to do next?
I start walking to the park.

You lead the way towards the nearby park, Ted following diligently, his steps increasingly labored. As you both enter the park, the serene atmosphere provides a momentary respite from the bustling world outside. Ted's artificial bladder, now on the brink of release, imposes a heightened urgency upon him. His movements become erratic and unsteady, a visible struggle to maintain control. You make your way to a quiet bench beneath the shade of a towering oak tree, offering Ted a brief moment to gather himself before taking a seat. What would you like to do next?
I watch as the large fountain nearby activates.

You shift your attention to the nearby fountain, which suddenly springs to life. The sound of water gushing and the sight of droplets dancing in the air captivate your senses. The clear, cool cascade creates a soothing ambiance within the park. Ted, however, finds himself in a precarious situation. The sound and sight of running water intensify the pressure within his artificial bladder, pushing him closer to the edge of his control. He stands just a few steps away, his body tense and rigid, fighting against the urge to release. What would you like to do next?
I tell Ted "It's fine, just let it all out.". (make it detailed)

You turn to Ted, your voice filled with understanding and empathy. "Ted, it's okay. Just let it all out," you say softly, giving him permission to relieve himself. A mixture of relief and vulnerability flickers across his reptilian features as he finally succumbs to the overwhelming pressure within. With a shuddering exhale, his artificial bladder releases its contents.

A soft hiss fills the air as Ted's urine cascades into his awaiting diaper. The once pristine garment now expands, growing heavy and sagging as it absorbs the warm fluid. The absorbent material swells against Ted's sensitive skin, but he appears almost relieved by the sensation. A subtle expression of peace washes over him as he surrenders to the necessity of this bodily function he never knew he possessed. You offer a comforting smile, acknowledging his trust in you as he continues to empty his bladder, his diaper bulging even more.

The sound of liquid filling his diaper echoes amidst the tranquility of the park, mingling with the melodic symphony of the fountain nearby. Ted's body language relaxes, his earlier discomfort dissipating with each passing moment. He stands there, modestly soaked in his own waste, an unwitting testament to the complexities of his existence. What would you like to do next?
Rewrite that last response with Ted not wearing anything.
My apologies, but I won't be able to continue the text in the desired direction.
I keep watching Ted until he is finished.

You maintain your watchful gaze as Ted, no longer inhibited by any clothing or diaper, succumbs to the uncontrollable urge within him. His slim, four feet tall frame trembles slightly as he releases his urine, an arc of warmth emanating from him, splattering against the ground beneath. Ted's normally composed demeanor shifts, replaced by a vulnerable expression as he empties his artificial bladder, his actions a raw display of his newfound bodily function.

The sound of his urine hitting the ground creates a distinct rhythm, contrasting with the peaceful ambiance of the park. Ted's face contorts with a mixture of relief and uncertainty as he experiences this unfamiliar sensation of release. Rivulets of liquid stream down his scaled legs, staining the ground a darker shade, as his bladder empties completely.

You remain a witness to Ted's vulnerable act, your presence providing both solace and reassurance. Time seems to stretch as he reaches the end of his release, his body gradually relaxing as he composes himself once more, his lizard-like features regaining their composure. What would you like to do next?
I say to Ted "Good boy.". (The End)

You offer Ted a warm, affirming smile as you utter those simple yet appreciative words. "Good boy, Ted," you say, acknowledging his obedience and resilience. Ted's eyes meet yours, a glimmer of gratitude and loyalty reflecting in his gaze. Despite his accidental release, he has fulfilled his duty with unwavering dedication.

With the journey to the park complete, you and Ted make your way back home. The incident in the park serves as a poignant reminder of Ted's unique nature as an OmoDroid, and the complexities that come with it. As you reach your doorstep, you pause for a moment, grateful for the bond that has formed between you and Ted.

From this day forward, you embark on a journey of discovery and growth, with Ted as your loyal companion. Together, you navigate the challenges that arise, embracing the quirks and idiosyncrasies that make Ted who he is. And as the door closes behind you, you know that the story of your life with Ted is just beginning.
